source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The front of a jet engine acts similarly to a diffuser, receiving air at 900 km/h,−5\circ\text{C}, and 50 kPa, bringing it to 80 m/s relative to the engine before entering the compressor. If the flow area is increased to 120% of the inlet area, find the temperature and pressure in the compressor inlet.

If the flow area is increased to 120% of the inlet area, find the temperature and pressure in the compressor inlet.
